shape_semantics: Joint-necessity. code over (cmp,vp,vpc,naive) = L,L,W,L: ONLY value_profile_cmplog resolves; cmplog, value_profile, and naive all block. Every branch carries TWO Pass-A signatures of ONE joint mechanism: an h0 (vpc>value_profile, attributed to I2S => I2S necessary) and an h1 (vpc>cmplog, attributed to CMP_MAP gradient => value_profile necessary). The shape itself (Layer 1) already asserts both techniques are jointly required; the evidence test does NOT re-confirm jointness (that would violate G2 by verifying vpc-resolves with vpc-resolves). Instead it discriminates WHICH joint subtype each branch is, on a measurement INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern: it reads the structural/value content of the cmplog-BLOCKING and value_profile-BLOCKING seed sets, OR the whole-corpus retention footprint of the gradient, and asks what the gradient adds OVER I2S-only (cmplog), and what I2S adds OVER gradient-only (value_profile).

hypotheses.label: vpc assembles a LARGER, multi-field structure than I2S-only (cmplog) ever reaches; the gradient is necessary to grow size/token-count past where exact substitution stalls, and I2S is necessary because the gradient alone (value_profile) places ~0 structural tokens
hypotheses.prediction: On the value_profile-BLOCKING seed set the structural-token count is ~0 (vp_tags ~ 0): the gradient alone cannot place ANY exact multi-byte structural token, so I2S is necessary. AND the vpc-RESOLVING median seed is materially BIGGER than the cmplog-BLOCKING median seed (size_lift >= 1.3): the gradient grows the structure past where exact-substitution-only (cmplog) plateaus, so the gradient is necessary. A pure-I2S-substitution mechanism (no assembly depth) would predict size_lift ~ 1.0 (vpc and cmplog reach the SAME-sized structure, cmplog just hasn't placed the one tag yet); a pure-gradient mechanism would predict vp_tags > 0 (the gradient could place tokens on its own). Only the assembly-depth joint predicts BOTH vp_tags~0 AND size_lift>=1.3.

hypotheses.label: vpc and cmplog reach a SIMILARLY-sized structure (size_lift ~ 1, sometimes vpc smaller), but vpc places a specific gradient-climbed VALUE that the cmplog-blocking seeds approach yet never reach; I2S is still necessary to place the surrounding exact literal(s), and the gradient is necessary for the last-mile value precision the structural proxy is blind to

params.comment: Built tool value_distance_reached (descriptor_key seed_set:value_distance_reached:operand_literal:per_branch). For the branch's decisive CMP site, take the target operand value (operand_literal from the signature, e.g. 0x00020000 for harfbuzz/5609-5616, the 'loca'/'post' tag for 5440/5442, the interned name3 string for libxml2/6414, the chained language/script tag for 6214/6223). The tool computes the MIN over each arm's seeds of (min over sliding windows of Hamming(window, target_operand)/width); numeric mode for inequality gates. vpc_min_distance is the vpc-RESOLVING arm, cmp_min_distance the cmplog-BLOCKING arm; distance_closure_ratio = (cmp_min_distance - vpc_min_distance)/max(cmp_min_distance, 1).
params.operand_source: signature.operand_literal / operand_width_bytes; cmp-site offset from the per-role coverage overlay or the analysis byte-diff (the W-vs-L divergent offset)
params.feasibility: BUILT + validated (anchor sqlite3/14437; 4/4 local anchors winner_closer=true). Seed sets for this hypothesis's harfbuzz + libxml2 members exist (286-rep DB); all scorable.

hypotheses.prediction: Structures are the SAME size (size_lift ~ 1.0, the assembly-depth proxy is blind here) BUT the vpc-resolving seeds reach a value strictly CLOSER to the target operand than the cmplog-blocking seeds ever do: cmp_min_distance > 0 (cmplog seeds approach but never hit the exact value) while vpc_min_distance ~ 0 (vpc lands it), so distance_closure_ratio is large. A pure-I2S mechanism would predict cmp_min_distance ~ 0 too (cmplog HAS I2S, so if a single substitution sufficed cmplog would also reach the value and resolve -- contradicting the L label); the fact that cmplog approaches-but-misses is exactly what the gradient (value_profile) supplies. This rules out 'I2S alone is enough' AND, because size_lift~1, rules out the assembly-depth story.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round 1). The cmplog-BLOCKING arm reaches a value that is STRICTLY CLOSER to the gate operand than naive/value_profile but still NON-zero, while the vpc-RESOLVING arm lands it (distance ~ 0): the value-profile CMP_MAP gradient supplies the last-mile closure on a fixed/string operand that cmplog's discrete I2S substitution stalls short of, and I2S is necessary because the gradient alone (value_profile) never reaches the operand window. Same-size structures (the assembly-depth size proxy is blind). This is the value-closure subtype the prior joint_value_precision rule under-captured because it cross-referenced size_lift (a different tool's metric) and used too strict a closure floor; this hypothesis scores entirely on value_distance_reached's OWN validated metrics.

params.comment: Reuses the BUILT value_distance_reached tool (descriptor_key seed_set:value_distance_reached:operand_literal:per_branch), scored ONLY on its own native metrics (no cross-tool size_lift dependency). Per-branch target operand from signature.operand_literal: harfbuzz/5609,5614,5616 -> 0x00020000 (width 4, derived_integer, numeric mode); lcms/2107 -> closest of {link,prtr,spac}; lcms/2184 -> closest of {link,abst}; lcms/2196 -> closest of {lams,link}; lcms/2209 -> closest of {lcms,link,prtr,spac,MCH1}; libxml2/7326 -> '<![CDATA[' (variable-length string, edit/prefix mode). All operands width>=4 so chance-alignment caveat (width<=2) does not apply.
params.operand_source: signature.operand_literal / operand_width_bytes; cmp-site offset from per-role overlay / analysis byte-diff (W-vs-L divergent offset, e.g. 0x18 version field for 5609/5614/5616, 0x0c device-class FOURCC for lcms).
params.feasibility: BUILT (value_distance_reached, validated). harfbuzz on-disk corpus on s4. lcms + libxml2 corpora are owned by server B (on-disk lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty); their resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds are in the 286-rep DB which is what the seed_set source consumes, so server B arbitrates them. Per cross-server rule these are NOT marked decidable:false for absence on s4.

hypotheses.prediction: The cmplog-blocking arm gets CLOSER to the operand than the gradient-less baselines yet stalls at cmp_min_distance > 0; the vpc-resolving arm closes it (vpc_min_distance ~ 0), so winner_closer == true and distance_gap > 0 with a substantial distance_closure_ratio. Discriminating: a PURE-I2S story predicts cmp_min_distance ~ 0 (cmplog has I2S; one substitution would land the fixed 4-byte constant / FOURCC and resolve -- contradicting the L label), so a non-zero cmp_min_distance with winner_closer rules out 'I2S alone is enough'. A PURE-GRADIENT story predicts value_profile reaches the window too; but vp blocks, so I2S-placed surrounding bytes are required. A pure ASSEMBLY-DEPTH story predicts winner_closer driven by a bigger structure, not a closer value -- here the structures are same-size and the gap is in the operand window, not token count. Only value-gradient-closure predicts winner_closer + non-zero cmp_min_distance + a same-size structure.
hypotheses.rule: winner_closer == true AND cmp_min_distance > 0 AND distance_closure_ratio >= 0.34

hypotheses.label: NEW (round 2). The value_profile CMP_MAP gradient is necessary as a CORPUS-RETENTION engine, not as a within-seed value climber: vpc ADMITS and HOARDS the near-miss intermediate inputs (operands progressively closer to the gate constant/structure) that bare cmplog's edge-only feedback discards because they do not flip a coverage bucket. The gradient's footprint is therefore a materially LARGER retained corpus in vpc than in cmplog -- the stepping-stone scaffold I2S then completes. I2S is necessary because the gradient-only arm (value_profile) still blocks (the L label): the scaffold is sterile without the exact-substitution stage that finally lands the operand. This is the subtype the seed-set-snapshot tools (value_distance_reached, joint_necessity) are STRUCTURALLY BLIND to: cmplog's discarded intermediates are absent from its FINAL seed set, so a final-snapshot distance/size comparison cannot see the retention difference; only a whole-corpus population count can. That is exactly why these branches failed every prior rule despite the cards uniformly attributing the cmplog block to 'no CMP_MAP gradient => near-miss inputs discarded'.

params.comment: BUILT tool bench/tools/corpus_size_ratio.py (descriptor_key corpus_queue:corpus_size_ratio:vpc_vs_cmplog:per_trial; not yet in the registry tools[] array but exists + has the i2s_homogenizes_corpus / ctx_corpus_inflation prior uses). It is a WHOLE-CORPUS, branch-INDEPENDENT comparison of two fuzzer ARMS over the target -- corpus_count_ratio = median(#saved seeds in the vpc queue) / median(#saved seeds in the cmplog queue) across trials 1-3. Here winner=value_profile_cmplog (the gradient+I2S arm that resolves), loser=cmplog (the I2S-only arm that blocks). The mechanism is RETENTION/INFLATION (the gradient admits more near-miss intermediates as new coverage), NOT homogenization, so composition_entropy_ratio is NOT required (requiring entropy<0.85 would be the wrong i2s_homogenizes_corpus story for a different shape).
params.operand_source: no per-branch operand needed -- the tool is operand-free and structural-token-free, which is precisely why it can score harfbuzz/5980 (USE category-range/sequence, no fixed operand) and libxml2/8161 (two-namespace structural-presence, operand_literal=null) that value_distance_reached and joint_necessity could not.
params.feasibility: BUILT. Needs on-disk corpora for BOTH arms (value_profile_cmplog + cmplog queues). harfbuzz arms on-disk on s4 -> 5609/5614/5616/5980 scorable on s4. lcms + libxml2 arms on-disk on server B (lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty) -> 2107/2184/2196/2209/6414/8161 scorable on server B. Per the cross-server rule, branches whose corpus is absent on the running server are NOT marked decidable:false -- the owning server arbitrates them.

hypotheses.prediction: The vpc queue retains a materially LARGER saved corpus than the cmplog queue: corpus_count_ratio (vpc/cmplog) >= 1.3 (the gradient admits the near-miss stepping-stones cmplog's edge-only feedback rejects). DISCRIMINATING (G1): a PURE-I2S story -- where the only delta vpc adds over cmplog is the (identical) I2S mutator and the gradient contributes NOTHING to corpus admission -- predicts corpus_count_ratio ~ 1.0 (both arms have I2S, both admit the same edge-novel seeds, the queues are the same scale); it CAN come out that way and would refute this hypothesis. A homogenization story (i2s_vp_LWLW, a DIFFERENT shape) would instead predict the entropy ratio collapsing while count stays flat -- here we predict count INFLATION, not byte homogenization. Only the gradient-as-corpus-retention mechanism predicts a corpus_count_ratio materially above 1 driven by retained near-miss intermediates. Because corpus_size_ratio reads ONLY saved-corpus population (never the blocker's resolve state), the prediction is independent of the L,L,W,L resolve pattern that defined the shape (G2-clean: a vpc arm could resolve the branch with a small corpus, giving ratio~1 and refuting).

hypotheses.label: NEW (round 1, honest inconclusive). Joint gates whose discriminating content is a CATEGORY-SEQUENCE / range gradient or a multi-token STRUCTURAL-PRESENCE condition with NO single fixed operand value to measure distance to, AND for which neither the token-count assembly proxy NOR the whole-corpus retention ratio fired -- no BUILT measurement can separate the I2S vs gradient contribution. Left honestly inconclusive (G3). This is the residual fallback bucket AFTER joint_gradient_corpus_retention has been tried: harfbuzz/5980 and libxml2/8161 are now FIRST routed to the operand-free corpus-retention test; they only land here if that whole-corpus ratio also fails to hold on their owning server.

params.comment: harfbuzz/5980: USE-machine case-10 transition gated by a RANGE comparison _keys[0] <= use_category() <= _keys[1] over a category-code SEQUENCE; operand_kind=enum_or_code, operand_literal=null. There is no fixed target value for value_distance_reached, no structural-token vocabulary for joint_necessity (no sfnt/FOURCC tags involved -- it is a per-glyph category prefix), and depth_reach measures iteration count not category-sequence content. libxml2/8161: 'second xmlNewNs on the same node' -- presence_or_nonnull on both h0 and h1, operand_literal=null; a multi-token structural-well-formedness condition (intact <?xml/<!DOCTYPE/xmlns chain) that the KEPT joint_assembly_depth (token_count) rule already scored and did not hold. Round 2 adds the operand-free joint_gradient_corpus_retention test (corpus_size_ratio) which CAN attempt both; this hypothesis remains the honest fallback ONLY for the case where the corpus-retention ratio also fails to hold. A relaxed-threshold re-test of the same token_count measurement is forbidden, and no other DIFFERENT built tool discriminates a structural-presence / range-sequence gate at the per-branch operand level.
params.feasibility: Fallback only. After joint_gradient_corpus_retention is tried (corpus_size_ratio, owning server), a branch that does not meet its ratio threshold has no further built measurement that discriminates its I2S-vs-gradient split -> decidable:false. The arbiter leaves such a branch inconclusive rather than force-fit a mechanism.

hypotheses.prediction: No falsifiable per-branch operand-level prediction can be made that a built tool could score: 5980 has no fixed operand for a distance metric and no structural tokens for a count metric; 8161's only per-branch measurement (token_count) already failed and cannot be relaxed. If the whole-corpus joint_gradient_corpus_retention ratio (tried first) does not hold for them on the owning server, honest decidable:false.

data_realities_respected: No seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence on an 'I2S event' is used (I2S is untagged in mutation_op).

No byte-alignment / byte_freq_ratio is used (joint gates have different-SIZED W vs L seeds); value content is measured via value_distance_reached (operand-window Hamming/edit distance, alignment-free), structure via struct_size_and_token_count, and the gradient's corpus footprint via whole-corpus corpus_size_ratio (a count/population comparison that needs no byte alignment).

value_profile is treated as a FEEDBACK throughout (its effect read via what the corpus reaches: token count, value distance, and -- new in round 2 -- the SIZE/POPULATION of the retained corpus, exactly the 'corpus admission' channel the data_realities name), never as a lineage gradient event.

joint_gradient_corpus_retention (corpus_size_ratio) reads ON-DISK corpora and needs BOTH the value_profile_cmplog and cmplog queues present; on-disk corpora exist only for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 on s4 and lcms/libxml2/libpng/bloaty on server B, so harfbuzz members are scored on s4 and lcms+libxml2 members on server B (cross-server: NOT decidable:false merely for corpus absence on the running server -- the owning server arbitrates).

Seed-set tools (joint_necessity, value_distance_reached) consume db/blockers.sqlite resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds (the 286 reps have them); cross-server, lcms+libxml2 seed-set members are arbitrated by server B.
notes:
ROUND-2 RE-DESIGN (loop-until-dry). SUPERSET / FREEZE: all FOUR prior hypotheses are carried forward VERBATIM (rules unchanged) so the 13 CONFIRMED branches re-validate -- KEEP: joint_assembly_depth [vp_tags<0.3 AND size_lift>=1.3], joint_value_precision [size_lift<1.3 AND cmp_min_distance>0 AND distance_closure_ratio>=0.5], joint_value_gradient_closure [winner_closer AND cmp_min_distance>0 AND distance_closure_ratio>=0.34]. The prior decidable:false bucket joint_sequence_or_structural_nondiscriminable is also retained (unchanged rule:false) as the residual fallback.

PRIORS KEPT (4): joint_assembly_depth, joint_value_precision, joint_value_gradient_closure (all three are the round-2 KEEP set, each confirmed >=1 branch), plus the decidable:false joint_sequence_or_structural_nondiscriminable fallback. None re-tested, relabeled, or dropped.

NEW MECHANISM (1 decidable): joint_gradient_corpus_retention, using the BUILT-but-not-yet-tried-here corpus_size_ratio tool. It targets all 10 still-unlabeled rule_not_met branches whose cards UNIFORMLY attribute the cmplog block to 'cmplog has I2S but no CMP_MAP gradient => near-miss intermediates are discarded / not retained as corpus' (harfbuzz/5609,5614,5616 exact 0x00020000 version; lcms/2107,2184,2196,2209 FOURCC/colorspace; libxml2/6414 name3; harfbuzz/5980 USE category-range; libxml2/8161 two-namespace). WHY they failed every prior rule: value_distance_reached and joint_necessity read the FINAL seed-set snapshot, but cmplog DISCARDS the near-miss intermediates -- so a final-snapshot distance/size comparison is structurally blind to a retention difference. The gradient's footprint is the RETAINED CORPUS POPULATION, not the value any surviving seed reached. corpus_size_ratio measures exactly that (median #saved seeds vpc/cmplog), which is also the data_realities-blessed reading of value_profile ('measurable only via corpus admission / what the corpus reaches (size...)'). WHY NEW (G1) and not a relaxed prior: (a) different TOOL (corpus_size_ratio, never tried on this shape) and a different MEASUREMENT CHANNEL (whole-corpus population, not per-seed value distance or token count) -- not a threshold relaxation of any already-scored measurement on these branches; (b) DISCRIMINATING -- a pure-I2S 'gradient adds nothing to corpus admission' alternative predicts corpus_count_ratio ~ 1 and refutes; a homogenization alternative (different shape) predicts entropy collapse not count inflation; (c) G2-CLEAN -- corpus_size_ratio reads only saved-corpus population, independent of the L,L,W,L resolve pattern (a vpc arm could resolve with a small corpus, giving ratio~1). BONUS: because corpus_size_ratio is operand-free and structural-token-free, it RESCUES harfbuzz/5980 and libxml2/8161 from the round-1 decidable:false bucket by giving them a first, falsifiable, scorable test -- they only fall back to inconclusive if the ratio also fails on their owning server.

BRANCH-INDEPENDENCE CAVEAT: corpus_size_ratio is a whole-corpus arm-pair measurement, so corpus_count_ratio is IDENTICAL for every branch of the value_profile_cmplog-vs-cmplog pair on a given target. This is acceptable for a JOINT-shape subtype label (the gradient's corpus footprint is a target+arm property, exactly the channel data_realities prescribe for value_profile), but it means this rule labels all qualifying unlabeled branches of a target with ONE shared verdict and cannot fine-separate them from each other. The decision_order protects the 13 confirmed branches: they match their KEPT, more-specific per-branch rules EARLIER in the order and never reach this whole-corpus test.

HONEST decidable:false RESIDUAL (<=2 branches): joint_sequence_or_structural_nondiscriminable = harfbuzz/5980 + libxml2/8161, but ONLY if joint_gradient_corpus_retention (tried first in the order) does not hold on their owning server. If the gradient-corpus-retention ratio holds, they are labeled; if not, they remain honestly inconclusive (G3) -- no built per-branch tool discriminates a range-sequence (no fixed operand) or a structural-presence (operand_literal=null, token_count already failed, relaxation forbidden) gate.

DECISION ORDER: joint_assembly_depth (most specific size+token conjunction) -> joint_value_precision (strict per-seed value closure>=0.5) -> joint_value_gradient_closure (per-seed native-metric closure>=0.34) -> joint_gradient_corpus_retention (whole-corpus population inflation>=1.3, the round-2 catch-all for the gradient-retention subtype) -> joint_sequence_or_structural_nondiscriminable (decidable:false residual). A branch matching none falls through to fallback:inconclusive. CROSS-SERVER: harfbuzz on s4; lcms+libxml2 on server B; corpus_size_ratio (on-disk) and the seed-set tools both have each branch scorable by its OWNING server, so no branch is marked decidable:false merely for corpus absence on s4.
